tables.description: This subject phenotypes data table contains subject IDs, study in which the subject participated in, demographic information such as subject's race, ethnicity, and gender, diagnostic information such as subject's overall response, treatment follow up information such as adverse events experienced by the subject, and other information such as the number of treatment cycles received by the subject.

variables.name: SUBJECT_ID
variables.description: Unique blinded ID for each patient

variables.name: Site
variables.description: The arm of EAY131 treatment

variables.name: Study ID
variables.description: Study name

variables.name: case.disease_type
variables.description: The type of malignant disease, as categorized by the World Health Organization's (WHO) International Classification of Diseases for Oncology (ICD-O).

variables.name: case.index_date
variables.description: The reference or anchor date used when for date obfuscation, where a single date is obscurred by creating one or more date ranges in relation to this date.

variables.name: case.primary_site
variables.description: The primary site of disease, as categorized by the World Health Organization's (WHO) International Classification of Diseases for Oncology (ICD-O).

variables.name: demographic.age_is_obfuscated
variables.description: The age or other properties related to the patient's age have been modified for compliance reasons.

variables.name: demographic.cause_of_death
variables.description: Cause of death

variables.name: demographic.days_to_birth
variables.description: Patient age at study entry in days

variables.name: demographic.days_to_death
variables.description: Number of days between the date used for index and the date from a person's date of death represented as a calculated number of days

variables.name: demographic.ethnicity
variables.description: Ethnicity

variables.name: demographic.gender
variables.description: Gender

variables.name: demographic.race
variables.description: Race

variables.name: demographic.vital_status
variables.description: The survival state of the person registered on the protocol.

variables.name: diagnosis.age_at_diagnosis
variables.description: Age at the time of diagnosis expressed in number of days since birth.

variables.name: diagnosis.best_overall_response
variables.description: The best improvement achieved throughout the entire course of protocol treatment.

variables.name: diagnosis.days_to_diagnosis
variables.description: Number of days between the date used for index and the date the patient was diagnosed with the malignant disease.

variables.name: diagnosis.metastasis_at_diagnosis
variables.description: The presence or absence of metastatic disease present at diagnosis.

variables.name: diagnosis.morphology
variables.description: Histology of neoplasm at study entry as categorized by the third edition of the World Health Organization's (WHO) International Classification of Diseases for Oncology (ICD-O-3).

variables.name: diagnosis.primary_diagnosis
variables.description: The patient's histologic diagnosis, as described by the World Health Organization's (WHO) International Classification of Diseases for Oncology (ICD-O).

variables.name: diagnosis.prior_treatment
variables.description: A yes/no/unknown/not applicable indicator related to the administration of therapeutic agents received before the body specimen was collected.

variables.name: diagnosis.site_of_resection_or_biopsy
variables.description: The anatomic site of the resection or biopsy of the patient's malignant disease, as described by the World Health Organization's (WHO) International Classification of Diseases for Oncology (ICD-O).

variables.name: diagnosis.tissue_or_organ_of_origin
variables.description: The anatomic site of origin, of the patient's malignant disease, as described by the World Health Organization's (WHO) International Classification of Diseases for Oncology (ICD-O)

variables.name: exposure.tobacco_smoking_status
variables.description: Smoking history at study entry

variables.name: follow_up.body_surface_area
variables.description: The 2-dimensional extent of the body surface relating height to weight.

variables.name: follow_up.days_to_follow_up
variables.description: Number of days between the date used for index and the date of the patient's follow-up appointment or contact.

variables.name: follow_up.ecog_performance_status
variables.description: ECOG Performance Status at study entry (integer values 0 to 5: only 0 and 1 are eligible)

variables.name: follow_up.height
variables.description: The height of the patient in centimeters.

variables.name: follow_up.progression_or_recurrence_type
variables.description: The text term used to describe the type of progressive or recurrent disease or relapsed disease.

variables.name: follow_up.recist_targeted_regions_number
variables.description: The number of baseline target lesions as described by the Response Evaluation Criteria in Solid Tumours (RECIST) criteria.

variables.name: follow_up.recist_targeted_regions_sum
variables.description: The sum of baseline target lesions, as described by the Response Evaluation Criteria in Solid Tumours (RECIST) criteria.

variables.name: follow_up.weight
variables.description: Patient weight in kilograms

variables.name: follow_up10.adverse_event
variables.description: The Common Terminology Criteria for Adverse Events low level term name for an adverse event.

variables.name: follow_up10.adverse_event_grade
variables.description: The intensity/severity of  an adverse event.

variables.name: follow_up10.days_to_adverse_event
variables.description: The number of days between the date used for index and the date of the patient's adverse event.

variables.name: follow_up10.days_to_follow_up
variables.description: The number of days between the date used for index and the date of the patient's follow-up appointment or contact.

variables.name: follow_up10.days_to_progression
variables.description: The number of days between the date used for index and the date the patient's disease progressed.

variables.name: follow_up10.progression_or_recurrence
variables.description: An indicator to identify whether a patient has had a new tumor event after initial treatment.

variables.name: follow_up10.progression_or_recurrence_anatomic_site
variables.description: The text term used to describe the anatomic site of the progressive or recurrent disease.

variables.name: follow_up33.adverse_event_grade
variables.description: The intensity/severity of  an adverse event.

variables.name: follow_up33.days_to_adverse_event
variables.description: The number of days between the date used for index and the date of the patient's adverse event.

variables.name: follow_up33.days_to_follow_up
variables.description: The number of days between the date used for index and the date of the patient's follow-up appointment or contact.

variables.name: follow_up33.progression_or_recurrence
variables.description: An indicator to identify whether a patient has had a new tumor event after initial treatment.

variables.name: follow_up33.progression_or_recurrence_anatomic_site
variables.description: The anatomic site of the progressive or recurrent disease.

variables.description: The individual agent(s) used as part of a treatment regimen.

variables.description: An indicator related to the administration of therapeutic agents received.

variables.description: The patient's final outcome after the treatment was administered.

variables.description: The kind of treatment administered.

tables.description: This subject consent data table contains subject IDs and consent group information.

variables.name: CONSENT
variables.description: Consent group as determined by DAC

variables.name: SUBJECT_ID
variables.description: Subject ID

tables.description: This subject sample mapping data table includes a mapping of study subject IDs to sample IDs. Samples are the final preps submitted for genotyping, sequencing, and/or expression data. For example, if one patient (subject ID) gave one sample, and that sample was processed differently to generate 2 sequencing runs, there would be two rows, both using the same subject ID, but having 2 unique sample IDs.

variables.name: SAMPLE_ID
variables.description: Sample ID

variables.name: SUBJECT_ID
variables.description: Subject ID
